name: Giardiniera Redo 2/18/94
recipeIngredient: 4 Green peppers (any color – red, yellow, etc.)
3 Chiles (dried) or 3 Tbsp dried chili powder substitute
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the carrots and cut them into half‑inch pieces.
2. Cut celery into similar sized pieces.
3. Remove seeds from peppers and cut into 1 ¾‑inch strips.
4. Break cauliflower into flower heads.
5. Dissolve salt in cold water; place vegetables in this brine, cover completely, and leave overnight (12–18 hours).
6. Drain the vegetables, rinse with cold water, and allow to drain again.
7. Select jars with glass or enameled lids. Wash jars thoroughly, then dry in a warm oven.
8. In a large glass or stainless steel pan, combine vinegar, mustard seed, celery seed, chiles (or chili powder), and sugar; bring to a boil for 3 minutes.
9. Add the carrots and onions, boil again for 5 minutes.
10. Fill warmed jars with hot vegetables, leaving at least a half inch of headspace. Top each jar with vinegar.
11. Seal lids firmly. Store in a 3 ½–4 quart jar; it’s as pleasing to look at as it is delicious.
